prompts: A cyclotron accelerates charged particles using a combination of an electric field and a perpendicular magnetic field. The structure consists of two hollow, semi-circular electrodes called "dees" positioned between magnetic poles that create a uniform magnetic field. An alternating electric field is applied across the gap between the dees, accelerating particles as they cross. The magnetic field forces particles into a spiral path, maintaining their circular motion, while their velocity increases each half-turn, causing them to spiral outward. This setup accelerates particles to high energies for applications in nuclear physics and medical treatments.
